4. Recommendation to Approve Out-of-Endorsement Assignment for Stacey Smith 207
Rationale: Washington State WAC 181-82-110 requires an out-of-endorsement assignment be approved by a
formal vote of the local school board.
Stacey Smith is an English and Social Studies teacher at Alki Middle School. She completed her bachelor’s
degree from California State University, Sacramento with a major in English. She holds an English
endorsement on her Washington State Continuing Teacher certificate. Stacey has taught English and Social
Studies for 22 years in Vancouver Public Schools. Under federal highly qualified requirements, Stacey was
deemed highly qualified in History due to her years of experience, and multiple college level and professional
development courses in History. Alki Middle School blocks English and Social Studies together, so while
Stacey does not hold a History or Social Studies endorsement, she is an ideal candidate to continue teaching
both English and History. The Board’s approval of an out-of-endorsement assignment will enable the District
to complete this placement.
Funding Source: N/A
Recommendation: That the Board of Directors approve an out-of-endorsement assignment request for Stacey
Smith.

12. Recommendation to Approve Contract Extension No. 2014-004-4 – Athletic Uniforms and Equipment 215
Rationale: On June 24, 2014, the Board of Directors approved the award of Bid No. 2014-004 for the purchase
of an unspecified quantity of athletic uniforms and equipment to be utilized by the Vancouver School District’s
(District) middle schools and high schools for athletic programs; including football, baseball, softball,
basketball, soccer, wrestling, volleyball, track, physical education, and cheerleading. The intent of this bid is to
establish convenience contracts with various athletic manufacturers and local distributors for the purchase of
all athletic uniform and equipment products. These convenience contracts allow District schools to purchase
any quantity of products with no minimum or maximum dollar limitations at the deepest discounts available.
The convenience contracts also allow schools to purchase athletic uniforms at the earliest possible order date,
thereby taking advantage of additional early order discounts. This recommendation constitutes the fourth year
of a five-year contract for the purchase of athletic uniforms and equipment for the 2017-2018 school year.

Description: Maxim Healthcare Services provides one or more licensed health care providers (i.e. RNs,
LP(V)Ns, and CNAs) who provide one-to-one nursing services to physically and/or mentally disabled VPS
students.

provided by a LPN, not to exceed eight (8) hours per day, and $50 per hour for services rendered by a RN, not
to exceed eight (8) hours per day.

Evaluation: VPS will closely monitor students’ progress toward IEP goals.

Vancouver Public Schools and Maxim Healthcare Services for one-to-one nursing services for two (2)
students for the 2016-17 school year, and authorize the president and secretary to execute the appropriate
documents.
